data.title: Tanco Holdings Berhad Reports Earnings Results for the First Quarter Ended September 30, 2025
data.description:
Tanco Holdings Berhad reported earnings results for the first quarter ended September 30, 2025. For the first quarter, the company reported sales was MYR 49.57 million compared to MYR 19.64 million a year ago. Net income was MYR 2.64 million compared to MYR 0.049 million a year ago.

Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was MYR 0.0004. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was MYR 0.0004.

data.title: Chin Hin Group Berhad Reports Earnings Results for the Third Quarter and Nine Months Ended September 30, 2025
data.description:
Chin Hin Group Berhad reported earnings results for the third quarter and nine months ended September 30, 2025. For the third quarter, the company reported sales was MYR 1,073.57 million compared to MYR 912.64 million a year ago. Net income was MYR 26.02 million compared to MYR 27.67 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was MYR 0.0074 compared to MYR 0.0078 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was MYR 0.0074 compared to MYR 0.0078 a year ago.
For the nine months, sales was MYR 2,980.04 million compared to MYR 2,256.87 million a year ago. Net income was MYR 65.63 million compared to MYR 78.76 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was MYR 0.0185 compared to MYR 0.0223 a year ago. Diluted earnings per share from continuing operations was MYR 0.0185 compared to MYR 0.0223 a year ago.

PPB Group Berhad Reports Earnings Results for the Third Quarter and Nine Months Ended September 30, 2025
data.description:
PPB Group Berhad reported earnings results for the third quarter and nine months ended September 30, 2025. Basic loss per share from continuing operations was MYR 0.1403 compared to basic earnings per share from continuing operations of MYR 0.1463 a year ago.
For the nine months, sales was MYR 4,089.36 million compared to MYR 3,955.28 million a year ago. Net income was MYR 456.11 million compared to MYR 854.21 million a year ago. Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was MYR 0.3206 compared to MYR 0.6005 a year ago.

data.title: PKO BP says Poland's Bank Guarantee Fund sets MREL requirement at 15.36% TREA
Mowi issues green bonds worth NOK 4.5 billion
data.description:
Norwegian salmon farming company Mowi has issued green bonds totaling NOK 4.5 billion. This is according to a press release.

The issue consists of two parts: bonds worth NOK 3.5 billion with a five-year maturity and an interest rate of three-month Nibor plus 110 basis points, and bonds worth NOK 1 billion with a five-year maturity and a fixed interest rate of 4.95 percent.

data.title: JPMorgan European Growth & Income outperforms benchmark at half year
data.description:
(Alliance News) - JPMorgan European Growth & Income PLC on Thursday noted its position as the "best performing investment trust in its sector", as it reported outperformance against its peers and benchmark during the interim period.

The London-based investment fund reported a total return on net assets per share of 14.2% for the six months that ended September 30, beating its benchmark, the MSCI Europe ex UK, which returned 10.7% over the same period.

The trust's net asset value per share grew 12.1% to 132.4 pence from 118.1p at March 31.

Chair Rita Dhut attributed stock selection as the primary reason for the period of outperformance.

The trust cited Heidelberg Materials AG as one of the main contributors to its strong performance, as it noted a "much more price rational" cement market owing to regulatory changes.

On the other hand, Carlsberg AS served as the main detractor as it printed weaker than anticipated volumes, particularly in Asia, said the company.

The trust noted that the company was voted the best investment company in the European sector at the AIC Investment Week Award ceremony held on Wednesday last week.

JPMorgan European Growth & Income reported an interim dividend of 2.4p per share, flat with the prior year.

Its shares were trading down 0.7% at 134.00 pence on Thursday afternoon in London.

Looking ahead, Portfolio Managers Alexander Fitzalan Howard, Zenah Shuhaiber and Tim Lewis said European valuations "remain attractive", noting that investment flows into these markets have "started to pick up".

They noted that they "continue to find stock picking opportunities that meet our criteria regarding valuation, quality and operational momentum."

By Christopher Ward, Alliance News reporter

Comments and questions to newsroom@alliancenews.com

Copyright 2025 Alliance News Ltd. All Rights Reserved.

data.title: Klarna Set to Take Off with Lufthansa Group, Bringing Flexible Payments to Travellers Across Europe and the U.S
data.description:
Klarna announced a new multi-market partnership with Lufthansa Group. The new agreement is facilitated by Klarna's integration with Adyen, the financial technology platform of choice for leading businesses. From November, Lufthansa Group customers will be able to choose Klarna's flexible payment options at checkout when booking travel experiences.

This new integration gives travellers greater control and convenience by offering the choice to pay in full, pay later, or spread the cost of their journey over time. The new options will be available first to customers in Austria, Belgium, Denmark, Finland, Germany, the Netherlands, Norway, Sweden, Switzerland, and the United States. This collaboration reflects the growing global demand for smarter, more flexible ways to pay for travel.

The rollout began in mid- November across Lufthansa Group's home markets and a selection of further important markets, with plans to expand across all Lufthansa Group Network Airlines, namely Lufthansa, Austrian Airlines, SWISS, and Brussels Airlines by the end of Second Quarter 2026. The partnership adds to Klarna's growing list of leading travel brands--including Airbnb, Expedia, Booking.com, and Hotels.com--as more travellers embrace fairer, more flexible ways to pay For their travel.

data.title: Wipro Limited to Power Odido Netherlands B.V.'s Digital Future Through AI-Enabled End-To-End IT Modernization
data.description:
Wipro Limited announced a multi-year engagement with Odido Netherlands B.V. to transform its IT landscape and enhance customer experience across their enterprise and consumer segments. By combining AI and deep consulting expertise, Wipro will help Odido improve customer engagement and satisfaction, improve productivity, and streamline operations to reduce costs. A key highlight of this multi-year engagement is the use of a self-funded model, where productivity-driven savings are reinvested to continuously fund new digital initiatives, ensuring that innovation remains both sustainable and scalable.

As part of the engagement, Wipro will lead a full-scale modernization of Odido's digital and enterprise technology landscape as well as drive IT simplification and automation.

data.title: Surge Battery Metals USA Inc. Announces Finalization of Terms of Joint Venture with Evolution Mining Limited
data.description: Surge Battery Metals Inc. through its wholly-owned U.S. subsidiary, Surge Battery Metals USA Inc. announced that it has settled all of the transaction documents in connection with its previously announced proposed transaction with a subsidiary of Evolution Mining Limited ("Evolution"), pursuant to which Surge US and Evolution propose to form a joint venture, for the purpose of continuing the development of the Nevada North Lithium Project (the "Transaction"). Surge US and Evolution intend to close the Transaction on December 1 and 2, 2025 following the re-opening of requisite government offices after the U.S. Thanksgiving holidays.

TotalEnergies, Repsol in Talks to Combine U.K. North Sea Assets, Cinco Dias Says, Citing Sources
data.description:
--France's TotalEnergies and Spain's Repsol are in advanced talks to combine their oil and gas exploration and production businesses in the U.K. North Sea, Spanish business newspaper Cinco Dias reports, citing unnamed sources.

--Repsol recently established a U.K. joint venture with Neo Energy, and TotalEnergies could join the business by contributing some of its assets, Cinco Dias reports.

--TotalEnergies and Repsol declined to comment to Cinco Dias.


Full story (in Spanish): https://tinyurl.com/3tjt5286


Write to Adria Calatayud at adria.calatayud@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

11-27-25 1032ET

MPS, Lovaglio Receives Notice of Investigation for Obstructing Supervision and Market Manipulation
data.description:
(Alliance News) - Banca Monte dei Paschi di Siena Spa announced on Thursday, in response to press reports, that it has received notification from the Milan Public Prosecutor's Office of a search warrant.

The company stated in a note that a notice of investigation has been served to Luigi Lovaglio in his capacity as Chief Executive Officer, and that the alleged offenses cited in the document pertain to obstruction of supervisory functions and market manipulation.

The company wrote that it is "confident it will be able to provide all necessary elements to clarify the correctness of its actions and expresses full trust in the competent authorities, to whom it confirms its complete cooperation."

Monte dei Paschi di Siena shares fell 4.5% to EUR 8.334 per share.

By Claudia Cavaliere, Alliance News reporter

Comments and questions to redazione@alliancenews.com

Copyright 2025 Alliance News IS Italian Service Ltd. All rights reserved.

Kongsberg Gruppen Signs EUR500 Million Air Defense Deal with Denmark
data.description:
The Norwegian defense company Kongsberg Gruppen has signed an agreement with the Danish government for the delivery of the NASAMS air defense system. The contract is valued at 500 million euros.

"With this procurement, Denmark will significantly enhance its operational capabilities and its ability to effectively counter modern airborne threats, while also further strengthening the overall presence of NASAMS air defense in the Nordic region," said Eirik Lie, CEO of Kongsberg Defence & Aerospace, in a press release.

The NASAMS system comprises Raytheon's radar and advanced missile variants, as well as Kongsberg's Fire Distribution Center (FDC) and multi-missile launchers.

data.title: Folksam Sells Swedbank Shares for an Additional SEK 111 Million
data.description:
Major shareholder Folksam sold another 380,341 shares in Swedbank between November 24 and 26. The shares were sold at an average price of SEK 292.10 per share, amounting to a transaction worth SEK 111.1 million. This information is detailed in the Swedish Financial Supervisory Authority's (Finansinspektionen) insider register.

Folksam has sold Swedbank shares on several occasions in October and November, following its announcement that it intends to gradually reduce its stake in the bank to about 5.5 percent over time.

As of November 21, Folksam owned 68.10 million shares, corresponding to 6.02 percent of the capital and voting rights in the company, according to the shareholder service Holdings. Folksam is Swedbank's second-largest shareholder after Sparbanksgruppen.

Rheinmetall not currently negotiating with VW over Osnabrueck site, CEO says
data.description:
BERLIN (Reuters) -Rheinmetall CEO Armin Papperger said on Thursday that the German defence company is not currently negotiating with carmaker Volkswagen about its factory in Osnabrueck.

Volkswagen said last year it was exploring alternative scenarios for the site's future use, raising hopes for a sale that could protect workers and save the carmaker restructuring costs.

Whether Rheinmetall invests in the factory depends on a special contract, he told a foreign journalists association.

"At the moment, we are not negotiating further because the plants we have currently established at Rheinmetall have sufficient capacity for the orders we have received and those that are in the pipeline," he said.

Papperger visited the factory earlier this year to determine whether it would be a good fit for a conversion to military production.

Rheinmetall has said that it would repurpose some of its automotive sites to mostly make defence equipment in an effort to move away from its loss-making civilian division and take advantage of a defence spending boom.

The company could also take over plants from automakers like Volkswagen - if the conditions are right, Papperger has previously stated.

Rheinmetall has been growing rapidly and investing in new plants and acquisitions as European countries put billions of euros towards defence following Russia's invasion of Ukraine.

The company is currently expanding its capabilities by acquiring the Luerssen Group's warship division, NVL, and establishing its own naval division.

Papperger said on Thursday that no further acquisitions to strengthen the naval business are currently planned.

(Reporting by Miranda Murray and Matthias Inverardi; editing by Matthias Williams and Jane Merriman)

India's Bandhan Bank to sell bad loans, write-offs via bid process
data.description:
(Reuters) -India's Bandhan Bank said on Thursday it will sell off its non-performing assets and written-off portfolios through a bidding process with asset reconstruction companies.

As of end-September, the lender's NPA portfolio had an outstanding principal worth 32.12 billion rupees ($359.4 million) and a written-off loan portfolio worth 37.19 billion rupees.

($1 = 89.3700 Indian rupees)

(Reporting by Hritam Mukherjee in Bengaluru)
